exam_traps.trap: Zero-copy cloning is ideal for creating dev/test environments from production
exam_traps.why_confusing: Cloning a production database for testing is instant and initially costs no extra storage. Changes in the clone don't affect production. This is the recommended approach, not full data copies.
exam_traps.correct_answer: Clone production DB for dev/test. Instant, no initial storage cost, fully isolated.

questions.question: After cloning a production database for testing, an Architect notices that scheduled tasks are not running in the cloned database. Why?

options.text: Cloned tasks are suspended by default and must be manually resumed.
The clone does not include task objects.
Tasks require a separate warehouse that wasn't cloned.

questions.overall_explanation: When cloning a database or schema, all tasks are cloned but are automatically suspended. This is a safety measure to prevent cloned tasks from accidentally running against production resources or consuming unexpected credits.

questions.question: What is the purpose of CREATE GIT REPOSITORY in Snowflake?

options.text: It creates a local Git repository inside Snowflake for version control.
It links an external Git repository so Snowflake can read files directly from it.
It enables Snowflake to push changes back to a Git repository.
It creates a backup of Snowflake objects in Git format.

questions.overall_explanation: CREATE GIT REPOSITORY links an external Git repository (GitHub, GitLab, etc.) to Snowflake. Files can then be referenced directly using stage notation: @my_repo/branches/main/path/file.sql. This is read-only -- Snowflake can fetch files but cannot push changes back.

questions.question: An Architect is designing environment isolation for a Snowflake deployment. Which approach provides the STRONGEST security boundaries between dev and prod?

options.text: Separate schemas within the same database.
Separate databases within the same account.
Separate accounts within a Snowflake Organization.
Separate roles with different object grants.

questions.overall_explanation: Separate accounts within a Snowflake Organization provide the strongest isolation. Each account has independent RBAC, network policies, and billing. A mistake in the dev account (like a bad GRANT) cannot affect production. Database-level isolation is good but still shares account-level settings.

questions.question: What happens to privileges when you clone a database using CREATE DATABASE ... CLONE?

options.text: All privileges are cloned along with the objects.
Privileges are NOT cloned by default; use COPY GRANTS to include them.
Only OWNERSHIP privileges are cloned; other grants are dropped.
Privileges are cloned but downgraded to read-only.

questions.overall_explanation: By default, privileges (grants) are NOT cloned. The cloned objects are owned by the role performing the clone. Use CREATE ... CLONE ... COPY GRANTS to carry over existing grants.
